Event Control
Knowledge to Action
• Methods to increase awareness of system events for better system control
• Cyber Security Application
© 2019 Joseph Kidd All Rights Reserved.
Natural Language Processing
• Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods are designed to work with data (like natural language)
that has not been explicitly parsed into a structured format.
• NLP explores the frequency and context of words in a large corpus of unstructured data, and in one
implementation, assigns numerical vectors to each word as a measure of that word’s relationship to
other words in the corpus.
• Converting words to vectors in this way allows the words to be graphed and their relationships across
multiple dimensions (contexts) explored.
• This allows a programmatic way to distinguish between: a “backdoor” that would call for a link to Home
Depot, and a “backdoor” that would call for a link to a cyber security resource
3
© 2019 Joseph Kidd All Rights Reserved.
Natural Language Processing Continued
• The conversion of words to mathematical vectors based on context also opens other possibilities for
using techniques from algebra and geometry to derive greater understanding.
• You can now realistically solve for statements like:
‣ France + Paris = United States + x
‣ With the NLP algorithm telling you that X=Washington
or the famous
‣ King/Queen = Man/X
‣ With the NLP algorithm telling you that X=Woman just based on matching the cosine values of the
angles between the vectors
4
© 2019 Joseph Kidd All Rights Reserved.
Current Natural Language Processing Project
• Given that the words of a sufficiently large unstructured corpus can be converted to vectors and
graphed
‣ How can we apply this to system event data?
‣ Can we avoid the expensive parsing efforts required for traditional correlation rules and instead
make determinations about normal/anomaly based on the geometry of the vectors on the graph?
‣ What else can we learn about events based on mathematical manipulation of their component
vectors?
